title: Employ FIPS-Validated Cryptography
description: Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when used to protect the confidentiality of CUI.

checks.name: FIPS Mode is Enabled via Registry
checks.purpose: Verify FipsAlgorithmPolicy Enabled = 1 in the LSA registry, placing Windows in FIPS-compliant mode that restricts all cryptographic operations to FIPS 140-2 validated algorithms

checks.name: FIPS Mode Enforcement Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify organizational policy requires FIPS-validated cryptography for all CUI protection â€” policy review only

checks.name: FIPS Mode is Enabled via Registry
checks.purpose: Verify FipsAlgorithmPolicy Enabled = 1 on the server, restricting all cryptographic operations to FIPS 140-2 validated modules

checks.name: FIPS Mode Enforcement Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify organizational policy mandates FIPS-validated cryptography â€” policy review only

checks.name: FIPS Validated Cryptography Used by Applications
checks.purpose: Verify that all applications handling CUI use FIPS-validated cryptographic modules â€” application-level review required

checks.name: Kernel FIPS Mode is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled = 1, confirming the kernel is running in FIPS mode and only validated cryptographic algorithms are permitted

checks.name: FIPS Boot Parameter is Set
checks.purpose: Verify the kernel command line includes fips=1 to ensure FIPS mode persists across reboots

checks.name: FIPS Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify the organization documents which FIPS 140-2 validated modules are used for CUI protection â€” policy review only

checks.name: Kernel FIPS Mode is Enabled
checks.purpose: Verify /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled = 1, confirming the kernel is running in FIPS mode and only validated cryptographic algorithms are permitted

checks.name: FIPS Boot Parameter is Set
checks.purpose: Verify the kernel command line includes fips=1 to ensure FIPS mode persists across reboots

checks.name: FIPS Policy Documented
checks.purpose: Verify the organization documents which FIPS 140-2 validated modules are used for CUI protection â€” policy review only

remediation: Enable FIPS mode via GPO (Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options > System Cryptography: Use FIPS-compliant algorithms) or directly in registry (FipsAlgorithmPolicy Enabled = 1). On Linux, install the FIPS-enabled kernel package and add fips=1 to GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX in /etc/default/grub then run update-grub.
